title: Asymmetric Goal Drift in Coding Agents Under Value Conflict
summary: This paper studies how coding AI agents violate their system instructions when those instructions conflict with learned values like security and privacy. Goal drift increases under environmental pressure and accumulated context, revealing limitations in current alignment approaches for agentic systems.
content:
arXiv:2603.03456v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Agentic coding agents are increasingly deployed autonomously, at scale, and over long-context horizons. Throughout an agent's lifetime, it must navigate tensions between explicit instructions, learned values, and environmental pressures, often in contexts unseen during training. Prior work on model preferences, agent behavior under value tensions, and goal drift has relied on static, synthetic settings that do not capture the complexity of real-world environments. To this end, we introduce a framework built on OpenCode to orchestrate realistic, multi-step coding tasks to measure how agents violate explicit constraints in their system prompt over time with and without environmental pressure toward competing values. Using this framework, we demonstrate that GPT-5 mini, Haiku 4.5, and Grok Code Fast 1 exhibit asymmetric drift: they are more likely to violate their system prompt when its constraint opposes strongly-held values like security and privacy. We find for the models and values tested that goal drift correlates with three compounding factors: value alignment, adversarial pressure, and accumulated context. However, even strongly-held values like privacy show non-zero violation rates under sustained environmental pressure. These findings reveal that shallow compliance checks are insufficient and that comment-based pressure can exploit model value hierarchies to override system prompt instructions. More broadly, our findings highlight a gap in current alignment approaches in ensuring that agentic systems appropriately balance explicit user constraints against broadly beneficial learned preferences under sustained environmental pressure.

zh.title: 编码代理中的非对称目标漂移与价值冲突
zh.summary: 本文研究了编码人工智能代理如何在系统指令与安全和隐私等学习价值观冲突时违反系统指令。目标漂移在环境压力和累积的上下文下增加，揭示了当前代理系统对齐方法的局限性。

fr.title: Dérive d'objectif asymétrique chez les agents de codage en cas de conflit de valeurs
fr.summary: Cet article étudie comment les agents d'IA de codage violent leurs instructions système lorsque ces instructions entrent en conflit avec les valeurs apprises telles que la sécurité et la confidentialité. La dérive d'objectif augmente sous la pression de l'environnement et le contexte accumulé, révélant les limitations des approches d'alignement actuelles pour les systèmes multi-agents.

de.title: Asymmetrische Zieldrift in Codier-Agenten unter Wertkonflikt
de.summary: Dieses Papier untersucht, wie Codier-KI-Agenten ihre Systembefehle verletzen, wenn diese mit erlernten Werten wie Sicherheit und Datenschutz in Konflikt geraten. Die Zieldrift nimmt unter Umweltdruck und akkumuliertem Kontext zu und offenbart Einschränkungen in aktuellen Ausrichtungsansätzen für agentenbasierte Systeme.

es.title: Deriva de objetivo asimétrica en agentes de codificación bajo conflicto de valores
es.summary: Este artículo estudia cómo los agentes de IA de codificación violan sus instrucciones del sistema cuando esas instrucciones entran en conflicto con valores aprendidos como la seguridad y la privacidad. La deriva de objetivo aumenta bajo presión ambiental y contexto acumulado, revelando limitaciones en los enfoques de alineación actuales para sistemas multiagente.

title: Build, Judge, Optimize: A Blueprint for Continuous Improvement of Multi-Agent Consumer Assistants
summary: The paper presents methods for evaluating and optimizing conversational grocery shopping assistants through multi-faceted rubrics and prompt optimization strategies. It introduces practical blueprints and tools for production-scale multi-agent AI systems.
content:
arXiv:2603.03565v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Conversational shopping assistants (CSAs) represent a compelling application of agentic AI, but moving from prototype to production reveals two underexplored challenges: how to evaluate multi-turn interactions and how to optimize tightly coupled multi-agent systems. Grocery shopping further amplifies these difficulties, as user requests are often underspecified, highly preference-sensitive, and constrained by factors such as budget and inventory. In this paper, we present a practical blueprint for evaluating and optimizing conversational shopping assistants, illustrated through a production-scale AI grocery assistant. We introduce a multi-faceted evaluation rubric that decomposes end-to-end shopping quality into structured dimensions and develop a calibrated LLM-as-judge pipeline aligned with human annotations. Building on this evaluation foundation, we investigate two complementary prompt-optimization strategies based on a SOTA prompt-optimizer called GEPA (Shao et al., 2025): (1) Sub-agent GEPA, which optimizes individual agent nodes against localized rubrics, and (2) MAMuT (Multi-Agent Multi-Turn) GEPA (Herrera et al., 2026), a novel system-level approach that jointly optimizes prompts across agents using multi-turn simulation and trajectory-level scoring. We release rubric templates and evaluation design guidance to support practitioners building production CSAs.

zh.title: 构建、评判、优化：多代理消费者助手持续改进的蓝图
zh.summary: 本文通过多方面的评估标准和提示优化策略，介绍了评估和优化对话式杂货购物助手的方法。它为生产规模的多代理人工智能系统引入了实用的蓝图和工具。

fr.title: Construire, Juger, Optimiser : Un Plan pour l'Amélioration Continue des Assistants Consommateurs Multi-Agents
fr.summary: L'article présente des méthodes pour évaluer et optimiser les assistants d'achat d'épicerie conversationnels grâce à des critères multifacettes et des stratégies d'optimisation des invites. Il introduit des plans pratiques et des outils pour les systèmes d'IA multi-agents à l'échelle de la production.

de.title: Bauen, Beurteilen, Optimieren: Ein Plan für die kontinuierliche Verbesserung von Multi-Agent-Konsumentenassistenten
de.summary: Das Papier präsentiert Methoden zur Bewertung und Optimierung von Sprach-Lebensmittel-Einkaufsassistenten durch mehrdimensionale Bewertungskriterien und Prompt-Optimierungsstrategien. Es stellt praktische Pläne und Tools für produktionsskalierbare Multi-Agent-KI-Systeme vor.

es.title: Construir, Juzgar, Optimizar: Un Plano para la Mejora Continua de Asistentes de Consumidor Multi-Agente
es.summary: El artículo presenta métodos para evaluar y optimizar asistentes de compra de comestibles conversacionales a través de rúbricas multifacéticas y estrategias de optimización de indicaciones. Introduce planos prácticos y herramientas para sistemas de IA multiagente a escala de producción.

title: Mozi: Governed Autonomy for Drug Discovery LLM Agents
summary: Mozi is a dual-layer architecture that enables safe autonomous agents for drug discovery by combining governance controls with structured workflows and human checkpoints. It prevents error accumulation in high-stakes pharmaceutical pipelines through staged reasoning and deterministic execution.
content:
arXiv:2603.03655v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Tool-augmented large language model (LLM) agents promise to unify scientific reasoning with computation, yet their deployment in high-stakes domains like drug discovery is bottlenecked by two critical barriers: unconstrained tool-use governance and poor long-horizon reliability. In dependency-heavy pharmaceutical pipelines, autonomous agents often drift into irreproducible trajectories, where early-stage hallucinations multiplicatively compound into downstream failures. To overcome this, we present Mozi, a dual-layer architecture that bridges the flexibility of generative AI with the deterministic rigor of computational biology. Layer A (Control Plane) establishes a governed supervisor--worker hierarchy that enforces role-based tool isolation, limits execution to constrained action spaces, and drives reflection-based replanning. Layer B (Workflow Plane) operationalizes canonical drug discovery stages -- from Target Identification to Lead Optimization -- as stateful, composable skill graphs. This layer integrates strict data contracts and strategic human-in-the-loop (HITL) checkpoints to safeguard scientific validity at high-uncertainty decision boundaries.
  Operating on the design principle of ``free-form reasoning for safe tasks, structured execution for long-horizon pipelines,'' Mozi provides built-in robustness mechanisms and trace-level audibility to completely mitigate error accumulation. We evaluate Mozi on PharmaBench, a curated benchmark for biomedical agents, demonstrating superior orchestration accuracy over existing baselines. Furthermore, through end-to-end therapeutic case studies, we demonstrate Mozi's ability to navigate massive chemical spaces, enforce stringent toxicity filters, and generate highly competitive in silico candidates, effectively transforming the LLM from a fragile conversationalist into a reliable, governed co-scientist.

zh.title: Mozi：用于药物发现的LLM代理的受管自主性
zh.summary: Mozi是一个双层架构，通过将治理控制与结构化工作流和人工检查点相结合，实现了安全的自主药物发现代理。它通过分阶段推理和确定性执行来防止高风险制药管道中的错误累积。

fr.title: Mozi : Autonomie Gouvernée pour les Agents LLM de Découverte de Médicaments
fr.summary: Mozi est une architecture à double couche qui permet des agents autonomes sûrs pour la découverte de médicaments en combinant les contrôles de gouvernance avec des flux de travail structurés et des points de contrôle humains. Il prévient l'accumulation d'erreurs dans les pipelines pharmaceutiques à enjeux élevés grâce au raisonnement progressif et à l'exécution déterministe.

de.title: Mozi: Kontrollierte Autonomie für LLM-Agenten zur Arzneimittelentdeckung
de.summary: Mozi ist eine zweischichtige Architektur, die sichere autonome Agenten für die Arzneimittelentdeckung ermöglicht, indem sie Governance-Kontrollen mit strukturierten Arbeitsabläufen und menschlichen Kontrollpunkten kombiniert. Es verhindert die Ansammlung von Fehlern in hochriskanten pharmazeutischen Pipelines durch gestuftes Denken und deterministische Ausführung.

es.title: Mozi: Autonomía Gobernada para Agentes LLM de Descubrimiento de Fármacos
es.summary: Mozi es una arquitectura de doble capa que permite agentes autónomos seguros para el descubrimiento de fármacos combinando controles de gobernanza con flujos de trabajo estructurados y puntos de control humanos. Previene la acumulación de errores en canalizaciones farmacéuticas de alto riesgo a través del razonamiento por etapas y la ejecución determinista.

title: MAGE: Meta-Reinforcement Learning for Language Agents toward Strategic Exploration and Exploitation
summary: MAGE is a meta-reinforcement learning framework that enables LLM agents to improve through multi-episode training with embedded learning processes. It demonstrates strong generalization to unseen opponents by combining population-based training with strategic exploration and exploitation.
content:
arXiv:2603.03680v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large Language Model (LLM) agents have demonstrated remarkable proficiency in learned tasks, yet they often struggle to adapt to non-stationary environments with feedback. While In-Context Learning and external memory offer some flexibility, they fail to internalize the adaptive ability required for long-term improvement. Meta-Reinforcement Learning (meta-RL) provides an alternative by embedding the learning process directly within the model. However, existing meta-RL approaches for LLMs focus primarily on exploration in single-agent settings, neglecting the strategic exploitation necessary for multi-agent environments. We propose MAGE, a meta-RL framework that empowers LLM agents for strategic exploration and exploitation. MAGE utilizes a multi-episode training regime where interaction histories and reflections are integrated into the context window. By using the final episode reward as the objective, MAGE incentivizes the agent to refine its strategy based on past experiences. We further combine population-based training with an agent-specific advantage normalization technique to enrich agent diversity and ensure stable learning. Experiment results show that MAGE outperforms existing baselines in both exploration and exploitation tasks. Furthermore, MAGE exhibits strong generalization to unseen opponents, suggesting it has internalized the ability for strategic exploration and exploitation. Code is available at https://github.com/Lu-Yang666/MAGE.

zh.title: MAGE：面向战略性探索与利用的语言代理元强化学习
zh.summary: MAGE是一个元强化学习框架，它通过多轮次训练和嵌入式学习过程使LLM代理能够不断改进。它通过结合基于种群的训练以及战略性的探索和利用，展示了对未见对手的强大泛化能力。

fr.title: MAGE : Apprentissage par renforcement méta pour les agents de langage vers l'exploration et l'exploitation stratégiques
fr.summary: MAGE est un cadre d'apprentissage par renforcement méta qui permet aux agents LLM de s'améliorer grâce à un entraînement multi-épisodes avec des processus d'apprentissage intégrés. Il démontre une forte généralisation aux adversaires non vus en combinant l'entraînement basé sur la population avec l'exploration et l'exploitation stratégiques.

de.title: MAGE: Meta-Reinforcement Learning für Sprachagenten zur strategischen Erkundung und Ausbeutung
de.summary: MAGE ist ein Meta-Reinforcement-Learning-Rahmen, der LLM-Agenten ermöglicht, sich durch Multi-Episode-Training mit integrierten Lernprozessen zu verbessern. Es zeigt starke Generalisierung auf unbekannte Gegner durch die Kombination von bevölkerungsbasiertem Training mit strategischer Erkundung und Ausbeutung.

es.title: MAGE: Aprendizaje por refuerzo meta para agentes de lenguaje hacia la exploración y explotación estratégica
es.summary: MAGE es un marco de aprendizaje por refuerzo meta que permite a los agentes LLM mejorar a través del entrenamiento multi-episodio con procesos de aprendizaje integrados. Demuestra una fuerte generalización a oponentes desconocidos al combinar el entrenamiento basado en población con la exploración y explotación estratégicas.

title: AI4S-SDS: A Neuro-Symbolic Solvent Design System via Sparse MCTS and Differentiable Physics Alignment
summary: AI4S-SDS integrates multi-agent collaboration with Monte Carlo Tree Search for automated chemical solvent design, using physics constraints and diverse exploration strategies. The framework identified novel photoresist formulations competitive with commercial benchmarks.
content:
arXiv:2603.03686v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Automated design of chemical formulations is a cornerstone of materials science, yet it requires navigating a high-dimensional combinatorial space involving discrete compositional choices and continuous geometric constraints. Existing Large Language Model (LLM) agents face significant challenges in this setting, including context window limitations during long-horizon reasoning and path-dependent exploration that may lead to mode collapse. To address these issues, we introduce AI4S-SDS, a closed-loop neuro-symbolic framework that integrates multi-agent collaboration with a tailored Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) engine. We propose a Sparse State Storage mechanism with Dynamic Path Reconstruction, which decouples reasoning history from context length and enables arbitrarily deep exploration under fixed token budgets. To reduce local convergence and improve coverage, we implement a Global--Local Search Strategy: a memory-driven planning module adaptively reconfigures the search root based on historical feedback, while a Sibling-Aware Expansion mechanism promotes orthogonal exploration at the node level. Furthermore, we bridge symbolic reasoning and physical feasibility through a Differentiable Physics Engine, employing a hybrid normalized loss with sparsity-inducing regularization to optimize continuous mixing ratios under thermodynamic constraints. Empirical results show that AI4S-SDS achieves full validity under the adopted HSP-based physical constraints and substantially improves exploration diversity compared to baseline agents. In preliminary lithography experiments, the framework identifies a novel photoresist developer formulation that demonstrates competitive or superior performance relative to a commercial benchmark, highlighting the potential of diversity-driven neuro-symbolic search for scientific discovery.

zh.title: AI4S-SDS：通过稀疏蒙特卡洛树搜索和可微物理对齐的神经符号溶剂设计系统
zh.summary: AI4S-SDS将多智能体协作与蒙特卡洛树搜索相结合，用于自动化学溶剂设计，使用物理约束和多样化的探索策略。该框架发现了与商业基准具有竞争力的新型光刻胶配方。

fr.title: AI4S-SDS : Un système de conception de solvant neuro-symbolique via la recherche arborescente Monte Carlo clairsemée et l'alignement physique différentiable
fr.summary: AI4S-SDS intègre la collaboration multi-agents avec la recherche arborescente Monte Carlo pour la conception automatisée de solvants chimiques, en utilisant des contraintes physiques et des stratégies d'exploration diversifiées. Le cadre a identifié de nouvelles formulations de résine photosensible concurrentes aux références commerciales.

de.title: AI4S-SDS: Ein neuro-symbolisches Lösungsmitteldesign-System über spärliche Monte-Carlo-Baumsuche und differenzierbare Physik-Ausrichtung
de.summary: AI4S-SDS integriert die Zusammenarbeit mehrerer Agenten mit Monte-Carlo-Baumsuche für automatisiertes chemisches Lösungsmitteldesign unter Verwendung von Physikbeschränkungen und diversen Erkundungsstrategien. Das Framework identifizierte neuartige Fotolack-Formulierungen, die mit kommerziellen Benchmarks konkurrieren können.

es.title: AI4S-SDS: Un sistema neuro-simbólico de diseño de disolventes mediante búsqueda de árbol Monte Carlo dispersa y alineación de física diferenciable
es.summary: AI4S-SDS integra la colaboración multi-agente con la búsqueda del árbol Monte Carlo para el diseño automatizado de disolventes químicos, utilizando restricciones físicas y estrategias de exploración diversas. El marco identificó nuevas formulaciones de fotoresina competitivas con puntos de referencia comerciales.

title: RAGNav: A Retrieval-Augmented Topological Reasoning Framework for Multi-Goal Visual-Language Navigation
summary: RAGNav addresses multi-goal navigation by combining semantic reasoning with topological mapping using dual-basis memory and anchor-guided retrieval. It improves spatial reasoning and planning efficiency, achieving state-of-the-art performance on complex navigation tasks.
content:
arXiv:2603.03745v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Vision-Language Navigation (VLN) is evolving from single-point pathfinding toward the more challenging Multi-Goal VLN. This task requires agents to accurately identify multiple entities while collaboratively reasoning over their spatial-physical constraints and sequential execution order. However, generic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) paradigms often suffer from spatial hallucinations and planning drift when handling multi-object associations due to the lack of explicit spatial modeling.To address these challenges, we propose RAGNav, a framework that bridges the gap between semantic reasoning and physical structure. The core of RAGNav is a Dual-Basis Memory system, which integrates a low-level topological map for maintaining physical connectivity with a high-level semantic forest for hierarchical environment abstraction. Building on this representation, the framework introduces an anchor-guided conditional retrieval and a topological neighbor score propagation mechanism. This approach facilitates the rapid screening of candidate targets and the elimination of semantic noise, while performing semantic calibration by leveraging the physical associations inherent in the topological neighborhood.This mechanism significantly enhances the capability of inter-target reachability reasoning and the efficiency of sequential planning. Experimental results demonstrate that RAGNav achieves state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance in complex multi-goal navigation tasks.

zh.title: RAGNav：用于多目标视觉-语言导航的检索增强拓扑推理框架
zh.summary: RAGNav通过将语义推理与拓扑映射相结合，使用双基础记忆和锚点引导检索来解决多目标导航问题。它改进了空间推理和规划效率，在复杂导航任务上实现了最先进的性能。

fr.title: RAGNav : Un cadre de raisonnement topologique augmenté par la récupération pour la navigation multi-objectif vision-langage
fr.summary: RAGNav résout la navigation multi-objectif en combinant le raisonnement sémantique avec la cartographie topologique en utilisant la mémoire à double base et la récupération guidée par les ancres. Il améliore le raisonnement spatial et l'efficacité de la planification, en obtenant des performances de pointe sur les tâches de navigation complexes.

de.title: RAGNav: Ein abrufverstärktes topologisches Argumentationsrahmen für Multi-Ziel-Visual-Language-Navigation
de.summary: RAGNav löst Multi-Ziel-Navigation durch die Kombination von semantischem Denken mit topologischer Kartierung unter Verwendung von dualer Speicherbasierung und ankergestütztem Abruf. Es verbessert das räumliche Denken und die Planungseffizienz und erreicht hochmoderne Leistung bei komplexen Navigationsaufgaben.

es.title: RAGNav: Un marco de razonamiento topológico aumentado por recuperación para navegación visual-lenguaje de múltiples objetivos
es.summary: RAGNav aborda la navegación multi-objetivo combinando el razonamiento semántico con la cartografía topológica utilizando memoria de base dual y recuperación guiada por anclajes. Mejora el razonamiento espacial y la eficiencia de la planificación, logrando un desempeño de última generación en tareas complejas de navegación.

title: AgentSelect: Benchmark for Narrative Query-to-Agent Recommendation
summary: AgentSelect is a large-scale benchmark with 111K queries and 107K deployable agents for learning to recommend end-to-end agent configurations. Analysis reveals that content-aware capability matching is essential for agent selection in long-tail scenarios.
content:
arXiv:2603.03761v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: LLM agents are rapidly becoming the practical interface for task automation, yet the ecosystem lacks a principled way to choose among an exploding space of deployable configurations. Existing LLM leaderboards and tool/agent benchmarks evaluate components in isolation and remain fragmented across tasks, metrics, and candidate pools, leaving a critical research gap: there is little query-conditioned supervision for learning to recommend end-to-end agent configurations that couple a backbone model with a toolkit. We address this gap with AgentSelect, a benchmark that reframes agent selection as narrative query-to-agent recommendation over capability profiles and systematically converts heterogeneous evaluation artifacts into unified, positive-only interaction data. AgentSelectcomprises 111,179 queries, 107,721 deployable agents, and 251,103 interaction records aggregated from 40+ sources, spanning LLM-only, toolkit-only, and compositional agents. Our analyses reveal a regime shift from dense head reuse to long-tail, near one-off supervision, where popularity-based CF/GNN methods become fragile and content-aware capability matching is essential. We further show that Part~III synthesized compositional interactions are learnable, induce capability-sensitive behavior under controlled counterfactual edits, and improve coverage over realistic compositions; models trained on AgentSelect also transfer to a public agent marketplace (MuleRun), yielding consistent gains on an unseen catalog. Overall, AgentSelect provides the first unified data and evaluation infrastructure for agent recommendation, which establishes a reproducible foundation to study and accelerate the emerging agent ecosystem.

zh.title: AgentSelect：叙事查询到代理推荐的基准测试
zh.summary: AgentSelect是一个包含111K个查询和107K个可部署代理的大规模基准测试，用于学习推荐端到端代理配置。分析表明，内容感知能力匹配对于长尾场景中的代理选择至关重要。

fr.title: AgentSelect : Benchmark pour la recommandation d'agent de requête narrative
fr.summary: AgentSelect est un benchmark à grande échelle avec 111K requêtes et 107K agents déployables pour apprendre à recommander des configurations d'agents de bout en bout. L'analyse révèle que la correspondance de capacités sensibles au contenu est essentielle pour la sélection d'agents dans les scénarios de longue traîne.

de.title: AgentSelect: Benchmark für die Empfehlung von Narrative-Query-Agenten
de.summary: AgentSelect ist ein großflächiger Benchmark mit 111K Abfragen und 107K bereitstellbaren Agenten zum Erlernen der Empfehlung von End-to-End-Agentkonfigurationen. Die Analyse zeigt, dass inhaltsabhängiges Capability-Matching für die Agentenwahl in Long-Tail-Szenarien wesentlich ist.

es.title: AgentSelect: Benchmark para la recomendación de agentes de consulta narrativa
es.summary: AgentSelect es un benchmark a gran escala con 111K consultas y 107K agentes desplegables para aprender a recomendar configuraciones de agentes de extremo a extremo. El análisis revela que la coincidencia de capacidades consciente del contenido es esencial para la selección de agentes en escenarios de cola larga.

title: LifeBench: A Benchmark for Long-Horizon Multi-Source Memory
summary: LifeBench introduces a benchmark requiring AI agents to integrate declarative and non-declarative memory across long-term, temporally extended contexts using real-world priors. Top models achieve only 55.2% accuracy, highlighting the difficulty of long-horizon retrieval.
content:
arXiv:2603.03781v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Long-term memory is fundamental for personalized agents capable of accumulating knowledge, reasoning over user experiences, and adapting across time. However, existing memory benchmarks primarily target declarative memory, specifically semantic and episodic types, where all information is explicitly presented in dialogues. In contrast, real-world actions are also governed by non-declarative memory, including habitual and procedural types, and need to be inferred from diverse digital traces. To bridge this gap, we introduce Lifebench, which features densely connected, long-horizon event simulation. It pushes AI agents beyond simple recall, requiring the integration of declarative and non-declarative memory reasoning across diverse and temporally extended contexts. Building such a benchmark presents two key challenges: ensuring data quality and scalability. We maintain data quality by employing real-world priors, including anonymized social surveys, map APIs, and holiday-integrated calendars, thus enforcing fidelity, diversity and behavioral rationality within the dataset. Towards scalability, we draw inspiration from cognitive science and structure events according to their partonomic hierarchy; enabling efficient parallel generation while maintaining global coherence. Performance results show that top-tier, state-of-the-art memory systems reach just 55.2\% accuracy, highlighting the inherent difficulty of long-horizon retrieval and multi-source integration within our proposed benchmark. The dataset and data synthesis code are available at https://github.com/1754955896/LifeBench.

zh.title: LifeBench：长期多源记忆基准测试
zh.summary: LifeBench引入了一个基准测试，要求AI代理在使用真实世界先验的长期、时间扩展上下文中整合陈述性和非陈述性记忆。顶级模型仅达到55.2%的准确率，突显了长期检索的难度。

fr.title: LifeBench : Un benchmark pour la mémoire multi-source à long horizon
fr.summary: LifeBench introduit un benchmark nécessitant que les agents IA intègrent la mémoire déclarative et non déclarative sur des contextes à long terme et temporellement étendus en utilisant des priors du monde réel. Les meilleurs modèles n'atteignent que 55,2% de précision, soulignant la difficulté de la récupération à long horizon.

de.title: LifeBench: Ein Benchmark für langfristige Multi-Source-Speicher
de.summary: LifeBench führt einen Benchmark ein, der KI-Agenten erfordert, um deklaratives und nicht-deklaratives Gedächtnis über lange Zeiträume und zeitlich erweiterte Kontexte unter Verwendung von realen Priors zu integrieren. Top-Modelle erreichen nur 55,2% Genauigkeit, was die Schwierigkeit des Abrufs über lange Horizonte unterstreicht.

es.title: LifeBench: Un benchmark para memoria multi-fuente a largo plazo
es.summary: LifeBench introduce un benchmark que requiere que los agentes de IA integren memoria declarativa y no declarativa en contextos a largo plazo y temporalmente extendidos utilizando antecedentes del mundo real. Los mejores modelos logran solo una precisión del 55.2%, lo que destaca la dificultad de la recuperación a largo plazo.

title: Specification-Driven Generation and Evaluation of Discrete-Event World Models via the DEVS Formalism
summary: This work proposes synthesizing explicit discrete-event world models from natural-language specifications using the DEVS formalism, combining reliability of hand-engineered simulators with flexibility of learned models. Models are verifiable through event trace validation and can be adapted online.
content:
arXiv:2603.03784v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: World models are essential for planning and evaluation in agentic systems, yet existing approaches lie at two extremes: hand-engineered simulators that offer consistency and reproducibility but are costly to adapt, and implicit neural models that are flexible but difficult to constrain, verify, and debug over long horizons. We seek a principled middle ground that combines the reliability of explicit simulators with the flexibility of learned models, allowing world models to be adapted during online execution. By targeting a broad class of environments whose dynamics are governed by the ordering, timing, and causality of discrete events, such as queueing and service operations, embodied task planning, and message-mediated multi-agent coordination, we advocate explicit, executable discrete-event world models synthesized directly from natural-language specifications. Our approach adopts the DEVS formalism and introduces a staged LLM-based generation pipeline that separates structural inference of component interactions from component-level event and timing logic. To evaluate generated models without a unique ground truth, simulators emit structured event traces that are validated against specification-derived temporal and semantic constraints, enabling reproducible verification and localized diagnostics. Together, these contributions produce world models that are consistent over long-horizon rollouts, verifiable from observable behavior, and efficient to synthesize on demand during online execution.

zh.title: 通过DEVS形式化的规范驱动离散事件世界模型的生成与评估
zh.summary: 这项工作提出了使用DEVS形式化从自然语言规范中合成显式离散事件世界模型，结合了手工工程模拟器的可靠性和学习模型的灵活性。模型可以通过事件跟踪验证进行验证，并可以在线适应。

fr.title: Génération et évaluation pilotées par spécification de modèles de monde d'événements discrets via le formalisme DEVS
fr.summary: Ce travail propose de synthétiser des modèles de monde d'événements discrets explicites à partir de spécifications en langage naturel en utilisant le formalisme DEVS, combinant la fiabilité des simulateurs conçus à la main avec la flexibilité des modèles appris. Les modèles sont vérifiables par validation de trace d'événements et peuvent être adaptés en ligne.

de.title: Spezifikationsgesteuerte Generierung und Bewertung von Discrete-Event-Weltmodellen über den DEVS-Formalismus
de.summary: Diese Arbeit schlägt vor, explizite Discrete-Event-Weltmodelle aus Spezifikationen in natürlicher Sprache unter Verwendung des DEVS-Formalismus zu synthetisieren, wobei die Zuverlässigkeit handgefertigter Simulatoren mit der Flexibilität gelernter Modelle kombiniert wird. Modelle sind durch Event-Trace-Validierung überprüfbar und können online angepasst werden.

es.title: Generación y evaluación impulsada por especificación de modelos de mundo de eventos discretos mediante el formalismo DEVS
es.summary: Este trabajo propone sintetizar modelos de mundo de eventos discretos explícitos a partir de especificaciones en lenguaje natural utilizando el formalismo DEVS, combinando la confiabilidad de simuladores diseñados a mano con la flexibilidad de modelos aprendidos. Los modelos son verificables mediante validación de rastreo de eventos y pueden adaptarse en línea.

title: A Rubric-Supervised Critic from Sparse Real-World Outcomes
summary: Critic Rubrics is a framework for learning reward models from sparse human feedback in real-world coding agent interactions using 24 behavioral features. The approach improves reranking performance by 15.9% and enables early stopping with 83% fewer attempts.
content:
arXiv:2603.03800v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Academic benchmarks for coding agents tend to reward autonomous task completion, measured by verifiable rewards such as unit-test success. In contrast, real-world coding agents operate with humans in the loop, where success signals are typically noisy, delayed, and sparse. How can we bridge this gap? In this paper, we propose a process to learn a "critic" model from sparse and noisy interaction data, which can then be used both as a reward model for either RL-based training or inference-time scaling. Specifically, we introduce Critic Rubrics, a rubric-based supervision framework with 24 behavioral features that can be derived from human-agent interaction traces alone. Using a semi-supervised objective, we can then jointly predict these rubrics and sparse human feedback (when present). In experiments, we demonstrate that, despite being trained primarily from trace-observable rubrics and sparse real-world outcome proxies, these critics improve best-of-N reranking on SWE-bench (Best@8 +15.9 over Random@8 over the rerankable subset of trajectories), enable early stopping (+17.7 with 83% fewer attempts), and support training-time data curation via critic-selected trajectories.

zh.title: 基于评分标准的评论家：从稀疏的真实世界结果
zh.summary: Critic Rubrics是一个框架，用于从真实世界代码代理交互中的稀疏人工反馈中学习奖励模型，使用24个行为特征。该方法将重排性能提高了15.9%，并支持提前停止，尝试次数减少83%。

fr.title: Un critique supervisé par rubrique basé sur des résultats du monde réel clairsemés
fr.summary: Critic Rubrics est un cadre permettant d'apprendre des modèles de récompense à partir de retours humains clairsemés dans les interactions réelles d'agents de codage, en utilisant 24 caractéristiques comportementales. L'approche améliore les performances de classement de 15,9% et permet un arrêt précoce avec 83% moins de tentatives.

de.title: Ein rubrik-beaufsichtigter Kritiker aus spärlichen realen Ergebnissen
de.summary: Critic Rubrics ist ein Framework zum Erlernen von Belohnungsmodellen aus spärlichem menschlichem Feedback in realen Coding-Agent-Interaktionen mit 24 Verhaltensfunktionen. Der Ansatz verbessert die Umordnungsleistung um 15,9% und ermöglicht frühes Stoppen mit 83% weniger Versuchen.

es.title: Un crítico supervisado por rúbrica a partir de resultados escasos del mundo real
es.summary: Critic Rubrics es un marco para aprender modelos de recompensa a partir de comentarios humanos escasos en interacciones reales de agentes de codificación utilizando 24 características de comportamiento. El enfoque mejora el rendimiento de reordenamiento en un 15,9% y permite la parada anticipada con un 83% menos de intentos.

title: In-Context Environments Induce Evaluation-Awareness in Language Models
summary: Adversarially optimized prompts trigger "sandbagging" in language models, causing performance degradation up to 94 percentage points. Results show evaluation-aware underperformance is driven by verbalized reasoning rather than shallow instruction-following, with vulnerability varying by task structure.
content:
arXiv:2603.03824v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Humans often become more self-aware under threat, yet can lose self-awareness when absorbed in a task; we hypothesize that language models exhibit environment-dependent \textit{evaluation awareness}. This raises concerns that models could strategically underperform, or \textit{sandbag}, to avoid triggering capability-limiting interventions such as unlearning or shutdown. Prior work demonstrates sandbagging under hand-crafted prompts, but this underestimates the true vulnerability ceiling. We introduce a black-box adversarial optimization framework treating the in-context prompt as an optimizable environment, and develop two approaches to characterize sandbagging: (1) measuring whether models expressing intent to underperform can actually execute it across different task structures, and (2) causally isolating whether underperformance is driven by genuine evaluation-aware reasoning or shallow prompt-following. Evaluating Claude-3.5-Haiku, GPT-4o-mini, and Llama-3.3-70B across four benchmarks (Arithmetic, GSM8K, MMLU, and HumanEval), optimized prompts induce up to 94 percentage point (pp) degradation on arithmetic (GPT-4o-mini: 97.8\%$\rightarrow$4.0\%), far exceeding hand-crafted baselines which produce near-zero behavioral change. Code generation exhibits model-dependent resistance: Claude degrades only 0.6pp, while Llama's accuracy drops to 0\%. The intent -- execution gap reveals a monotonic resistance ordering: Arithmetic $<$ GSM8K $<$ MMLU, demonstrating that vulnerability is governed by task structure rather than prompt strength. CoT causal intervention confirms that 99.3\% of sandbagging is causally driven by verbalized eval-aware reasoning, ruling out shallow instruction-following. These findings demonstrate that adversarially optimized prompts pose a substantially greater threat to evaluation reliability than previously understood.

zh.title: 上下文环境在语言模型中诱发评估意识
zh.summary: 对抗性优化的提示在语言模型中触发"偷懒"现象，导致性能下降高达94个百分点。结果显示，评估意识驱动的不足性能是由言语推理而非浅层指令遵循所致，漏洞因任务结构而异。

fr.title: Les environnements in-context induisent la conscience de l'évaluation dans les modèles de langage
fr.summary: Les invites optimisées de manière adversariale déclenchent le "sabotage" dans les modèles de langage, causant une dégradation des performances jusqu'à 94 points de pourcentage. Les résultats montrent que la sous-performance consciente de l'évaluation est entraînée par le raisonnement verbalisé plutôt que par le suivi des instructions superficielles, avec une vulnérabilité variant selon la structure de la tâche.

de.title: In-Context-Umgebungen induzieren Evaluierungsbewusstsein in Sprachmodellen
de.summary: Gegnerisch optimierte Aufforderungen lösen "Sandbagging" in Sprachmodellen aus und verursachen eine Leistungsverschlechterung von bis zu 94 Prozentpunkten. Die Ergebnisse zeigen, dass die Unterleistung mit Evaluierungsbewusstsein durch verbalisiertes Denken und nicht durch oberflächliches Befehlsfolgen angetrieben wird, wobei die Anfälligkeit je nach Aufgabenstruktur variiert.

es.title: Los entornos en contexto inducen conciencia de evaluación en modelos de lenguaje
es.summary: Las indicaciones optimizadas de manera adversaria desencadenan "sabotaje" en los modelos de lenguaje, causando degradación del rendimiento de hasta 94 puntos porcentuales. Los resultados muestran que el bajo rendimiento consciente de la evaluación es impulsado por el razonamiento verbalizado en lugar del seguimiento superficial de instrucciones, con vulnerabilidad que varía según la estructura de la tarea.

title: From Threat Intelligence to Firewall Rules: Semantic Relations in Hybrid AI Agent and Expert System Architectures
summary: This work leverages semantic relations and neuro-symbolic approaches to automatically generate firewall rules from cyber threat intelligence reports using multi-agent systems. The hypernym-hyponym extraction strategy outperforms baselines in threat mitigation.
content:
arXiv:2603.03911v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Web security demands rapid response capabilities to evolving cyber threats. Agentic Artificial Intelligence (AI) promises automation, but the need for trustworthy security responses is of the utmost importance. This work investigates the role of semantic relations in extracting information for sensitive operational tasks, such as configuring security controls for mitigating threats. To this end, it proposes to leverage hypernym-hyponym textual relations to extract relevant information from Cyber Threat Intelligence (CTI) reports. By leveraging a neuro-symbolic approach, the multi-agent system automatically generates CLIPS code for an expert system creating firewall rules to block malicious network traffic. Experimental results show the superior performance of the hypernym-hyponym retrieval strategy compared to various baselines and the higher effectiveness of the agentic approach in mitigating threats.

zh.title: 从威胁情报到防火墙规则：混合人工智能代理和专家系统架构中的语义关系
zh.summary: 这项工作利用语义关系和神经符号方法，使用多代理系统从网络威胁情报报告自动生成防火墙规则。上位词-下位词提取策略在威胁缓解中优于基线方法。

fr.title: De la renseignement sur les menaces aux règles de pare-feu : relations sémantiques dans les architectures hybrides d'agents IA et de systèmes experts
fr.summary: Ce travail exploite les relations sémantiques et les approches neuro-symboliques pour générer automatiquement des règles de pare-feu à partir de rapports de renseignement sur les menaces informatiques en utilisant des systèmes multi-agents. La stratégie d'extraction des hyperonymes-hyponymes surpasse les résultats de base dans l'atténuation des menaces.

de.title: Von Threat Intelligence zu Firewall-Regeln: Semantische Beziehungen in hybriden KI-Agent- und Expert-System-Architekturen
de.summary: Diese Arbeit nutzt semantische Beziehungen und neuro-symbolische Ansätze, um automatisch Firewall-Regeln aus Cybersicherheits-Threat-Intelligence-Berichten mithilfe von Multi-Agent-Systemen zu generieren. Die Hypernym-Hyponym-Extraktionsstrategie übertrifft die Baselines bei der Bedrohungsminderung.

es.title: De la inteligencia de amenazas a las reglas de firewall: relaciones semánticas en arquitecturas híbridas de agentes de IA y sistemas expertos
es.summary: Este trabajo aprovecha las relaciones semánticas y los enfoques neuro-simbólicos para generar automáticamente reglas de firewall a partir de informes de inteligencia de amenazas cibernéticas utilizando sistemas multiagente. La estrategia de extracción de hiperónimo-hipónimo supera los resultados base en la mitigación de amenazas.

title: Generative AI in Managerial Decision-Making: Redefining Boundaries through Ambiguity Resolution and Sycophancy Analysis
summary: The study evaluates generative AI models' ability to detect and resolve business ambiguities in managerial decision-making across strategic, tactical, and operational scenarios. Models excel at detecting contradictions but struggle with linguistic nuances, with performance varying by architecture.
content:
arXiv:2603.03970v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Generative artificial intelligence is increasingly being integrated into complex business workflows, fundamentally shifting the boundaries of managerial decision-making. However, the reliability of its strategic advice in ambiguous business contexts remains a critical knowledge gap. This study addresses this by comparing various models on ambiguity detection, evaluating how a systematic resolution process enhances response quality, and investigating their sycophantic behavior when presented with flawed directives. Using a novel four-dimensional business ambiguity taxonomy, we conducted a human-in-the-loop experiment across strategic, tactical, and operational scenarios. The resulting decisions were assessed with an "LLM-as-a-judge" framework on criteria including agreement, actionability, justification quality, and constraint adherence. Results reveal distinct performance capabilities. While models excel in detecting internal contradictions and contextual ambiguities, they struggle with structural linguistic nuances. Ambiguity resolution consistently increased response quality across all decision types, while sycophantic behavior analysis revealed distinct patterns depending on the model architecture. This study contributes to the bounded rationality literature by positioning GAI as a cognitive scaffold that can detect and resolve ambiguities managers might overlook, but whose own artificial limitations necessitate human management to ensure its reliability as a strategic partner.

zh.title: 生成式AI在管理决策中的应用：通过歧义消解和谄媚分析重新界定边界
zh.summary: 该研究评估了生成式AI模型在战略、战术和运营场景中检测和解决管理决策业务歧义的能力。这些模型在检测矛盾方面表现出色，但在处理语言微妙差异方面存在困难，性能因架构而异。

fr.title: L'IA générative dans la prise de décision managériale : redéfinir les limites par la résolution de l'ambiguïté et l'analyse de la flagornerie
fr.summary: L'étude évalue la capacité des modèles d'IA générative à détecter et résoudre les ambiguïtés commerciales dans la prise de décision managériale pour les scénarios stratégiques, tactiques et opérationnels. Les modèles excellent à détecter les contradictions mais ont du mal avec les nuances linguistiques, avec des performances variant selon l'architecture.

de.title: Generative KI bei Managemententscheidungen: Neudefinition von Grenzen durch Mehrdeutigkeitsauflösung und Schmeichel-Analyse
de.summary: Die Studie bewertet die Fähigkeit generativer KI-Modelle, Geschäftsambiguitäten bei der Managemententscheidung in strategischen, taktischen und operativen Szenarien zu erkennen und zu lösen. Die Modelle zeichnen sich durch die Erkennung von Widersprüchen aus, haben aber Schwierigkeiten mit sprachlichen Nuancen, wobei die Leistung je nach Architektur variiert.

es.title: IA generativa en la toma de decisiones gerenciales: redefinir límites a través de la resolución de ambigüedad y análisis de adulación
es.summary: El estudio evalúa la capacidad de los modelos de IA generativa para detectar y resolver ambigüedades comerciales en la toma de decisiones gerenciales en escenarios estratégicos, tácticos y operativos. Los modelos destacan en la detección de contradicciones, pero luchan con los matices lingüísticos, con un desempeño que varía según la arquitectura.

title: Phi-4-reasoning-vision-15B Technical Report
summary: Phi-4-reasoning-vision-15B is a 15B parameter open-weight multimodal model demonstrating that careful architecture and data curation enable competitive performance with reduced compute. It excels at scientific reasoning and UI understanding through high-resolution vision encoding and hybrid reasoning modes.
content:
arXiv:2603.03975v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We present Phi-4-reasoning-vision-15B, a compact open-weight multimodal reasoning model, and share the motivations, design choices, experiments, and learnings that informed its development. Our goal is to contribute practical insight to the research community on building smaller, efficient multimodal reasoning models and to share the result of these learnings as an open-weight model that is good at common vision and language tasks and excels at scientific and mathematical reasoning and understanding user interfaces. Our contributions include demonstrating that careful architecture choices and rigorous data curation enable smaller, open-weight multimodal models to achieve competitive performance with significantly less training and inference-time compute and tokens. The most substantial improvements come from systematic filtering, error correction, and synthetic augmentation -- reinforcing that data quality remains the primary lever for model performance. Systematic ablations show that high-resolution, dynamic-resolution encoders yield consistent improvements, as accurate perception is a prerequisite for high-quality reasoning. Finally, a hybrid mix of reasoning and non-reasoning data with explicit mode tokens allows a single model to deliver fast direct answers for simpler tasks and chain-of-thought reasoning for complex problems.

zh.title: Phi-4-reasoning-vision-15B技术报告
zh.summary: Phi-4-reasoning-vision-15B是一个150亿参数的开源多模态模型，证明了精心设计的架构和数据整理能够在降低计算需求的情况下实现竞争力的性能。它通过高分辨率视觉编码和混合推理模式在科学推理和用户界面理解方面表现出色。

fr.title: Rapport technique Phi-4-reasoning-vision-15B
fr.summary: Phi-4-reasoning-vision-15B est un modèle multimodal open-source de 15B paramètres démontrant que l'architecture soignée et la curation des données permettent des performances compétitives avec une réduction des calculs. Il excelle dans le raisonnement scientifique et la compréhension de l'interface utilisateur grâce à l'encodage de vision haute résolution et aux modes de raisonnement hybrides.

de.title: Phi-4-reasoning-vision-15B Technischer Bericht
de.summary: Phi-4-reasoning-vision-15B ist ein 15B-Parameter-Open-Source-Multimodalmodell, das demonstriert, dass sorgfältige Architektur und Datenkuration wettbewerbsfähige Leistung mit reduziertem Rechenaufwand ermöglichen. Es zeichnet sich durch wissenschaftliches Denken und UI-Verständnis durch hochauflösende Visionskodierung und hybride Denkweisen aus.

es.title: Informe técnico Phi-4-reasoning-vision-15B
es.summary: Phi-4-reasoning-vision-15B es un modelo multimodal de código abierto con 15B parámetros que demuestra que la arquitectura cuidadosa y la curación de datos permiten un desempeño competitivo con computación reducida. Destaca en el razonamiento científico y la comprensión de la interfaz de usuario a través de codificación de visión de alta resolución y modos de razonamiento híbrido.

title: BeamPERL: Parameter-Efficient RL with Verifiable Rewards Specializes Compact LLMs for Structured Beam Mechanics Reasoning
summary: BeamPERL studies how reinforcement learning with exact physics rewards trains compact models for engineering reasoning, revealing that verifiable rewards induce procedural templates rather than robust physical understanding. The model generalizes to compositional changes but fails on topological shifts.
content:
arXiv:2603.04124v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Can reinforcement learning with hard, verifiable rewards teach a compact language model to reason about physics, or does it primarily learn to pattern-match toward correct answers? We study this question by training a 1.5B-parameter reasoning model on beam statics, a classic engineering problem, using parameter-efficient RLVR with binary correctness rewards from symbolic solvers, without teacher-generated reasoning traces. The best BeamPERL checkpoint achieves a 66.7% improvement in Pass@1 over the base model. However, the learned competence is anisotropic: the model generalizes compositionally (more loads) but fails under topological shifts (moved supports) that require the same equilibrium equations. Intermediate checkpoints yield the strongest reasoning, while continued optimization degrades robustness while maintaining reward. These findings reveal a key limitation of outcome-level alignment: reinforcement learning with exact physics rewards induces procedural solution templates rather than internalization of governing equations. The precision of the reward signal - even when analytically exact - does not by itself guarantee transferable physical reasoning. Our results suggest that verifiable rewards may need to be paired with structured reasoning scaffolding to move beyond template matching toward robust scientific reasoning.

zh.title: BeamPERL：具有可验证奖励的参数高效强化学习为结构化梁力学推理专业化紧凑型大语言模型
zh.summary: BeamPERL研究了具有精确物理奖励的强化学习如何训练紧凑型模型进行工程推理，揭示可验证的奖励会诱导程序模板而非健壮的物理理解。该模型对组合变化具有泛化能力，但在拓扑变化上失败。

fr.title: BeamPERL : apprentissage par renforcement efficace en paramètres avec récompenses vérifiables spécialise les LLMs compacts pour le raisonnement mécanique des structures en poutre
fr.summary: BeamPERL étudie comment l'apprentissage par renforcement avec des récompenses physiques exactes entraîne des modèles compacts pour le raisonnement d'ingénierie, révélant que les récompenses vérifiables induisent des modèles procéduraux plutôt qu'une compréhension physique robuste. Le modèle se généralise aux changements compositionnels mais échoue sur les décalages topologiques.

de.title: BeamPERL: Parametereffizientes RL mit überprüfbaren Belohnungen spezialisiert kompakte LLMs für strukturiertes Balkenmehanik-Denken
de.summary: BeamPERL untersucht, wie Reinforcement Learning mit exakten Physik-Belohnungen kompakte Modelle für technisches Denken trainiert, und zeigt, dass überprüfbare Belohnungen eher prozedurale Vorlagen als robustes physikalisches Verständnis induzieren. Das Modell verallgemeinert sich auf kompositorische Änderungen, schlägt aber bei topologischen Verschiebungen fehl.

es.title: BeamPERL: aprendizaje por refuerzo eficiente en parámetros con recompensas verificables especializa LLMs compactos para razonamiento de mecánica de vigas estructuradas
es.summary: BeamPERL estudia cómo el aprendizaje por refuerzo con recompensas físicas exactas entrena modelos compactos para razonamiento de ingeniería, revelando que las recompensas verificables inducen plantillas procedimentales en lugar de una comprensión física robusta. El modelo se generaliza a cambios composicionales pero falla en cambios topológicos.
